mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of setup for distributed systems.

steps.title: Install the vLLM CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface, which is necessary for managing vLLM deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.16.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new GKE cluster with the specified version and node count, which is required to run vLLM.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to communicate with your GKE cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys vLLM using Helm, which simplifies the management of Kubernetes applications.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.16.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.16.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This enables autoscaling for the vLLM deployment.
> This sets up monitoring for vLLM.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This command removes the vLLM deployment from your cluster. Note that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This removes the vLLM release.
> This deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.16.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Upgrade to the latest version of vLLM.
> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name to allow scheduling again.
> Verify that all pods are running and ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the image tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod may be failing to start due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the request to the vLLM service is timing out.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure the pods are running properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests for the vLLM deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new quantization methods and improved memory management with PagedAttention.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and a suitable cloud environment.
